content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/monitoring-plugin/source/web/src/components/console/console-shared/src/components/empty-state/AccessDenied.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 2 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 24
Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-u-text-align-left]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-u-text-align-left' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-u-text-align-left' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:
  matchingText: pf-v5-u-text-align-left

Code context:
```
   23            <FlexItem>
   24              <Alert variant="danger" className="pf-v5-u-text-align-left" title={t('Error details')}>
   25                {children}

```

### Fix 2
Line: 24
Rule [semver-css-class-removed-u-text-align-left]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-u-text-align-left' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-u-text-align-left' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Incident context:
  matchingText: pf-v5-u-text-align-left

Code context:
```
   23            <FlexItem>
   24              <Alert variant="danger" className="pf-v5-u-text-align-left" title={t('Error details')}>
   25                {children}

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/monitoring-plugin/source/web/src/components/console/console-shared/src/components/empty-state/AccessDenied.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me reason through the fixes:

**Fix 1 & Fix 2** both target the same thing: the CSS class `pf-v5-u-text-align-left` on line 24. Both fixes say this class was removed and has no equivalent in PF v6. The recommendation is to remove the class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Since `pf-v5-u-text-align-left` is a utility class for `text-align: left`, and `text-align: left` is typically the default text alignment, removing it is the safest approach. The Alert component's text will naturally align left by default.

Since `className="pf-v5-u-text-align-left"` is the only class in the `className` prop, I should remove the entire `className` prop rather than leaving an empty string.

Let me apply the fix:

The file looks correct. No imports became unused, and no other artifacts need cleanup.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Detail |
|-----|------|--------|--------|
| Fix 1 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-u-text-align-left` | ✅ Applied | Removed the `className="pf-v5-u-text-align-left"` prop from the `<Alert>` on line 24. The class was removed in PF v6 with no equivalent. Since `text-align: left` is the default alignment, removing the class preserves the existing visual behavior. |
| Fix 2 | `semver-css-class-removed-u-text-align-left` | ✅ Applied | Same change as Fix 1 — both fixes targeted the identical class on the same line. Resolved by the single edit above. |
